text: Honey never spoils. Archaeologists have found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still perfectly edible.
Octopuses have three hearts: two pump blood to the gills, and one pumps it to the rest of the body.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus. It takes 243 Earth days to rotate once but only 225 Earth days to orbit the Sun.
The human nose can detect over one trillion distinct scents.
Bananas are naturally radioactive because they contain potassium-40.
The Great Wall of China is not a single continuous wall but a series of walls and fortifications built over centuries by different dynasties.
Lake Baikal in Russia holds about 20% of the world's unfrozen surface fresh water.
The first computer programmer was Ada Lovelace, who wrote an algorithm for Charles Babbage's Analytical Engine in 1843.
There are more possible iterations of a game of chess than there are atoms in the observable universe.
The shortest war in history was between Britain and Zanzibar on August 27, 1896, lasting only 38 to 45 minutes.
A group of flamingos is called a 'flamboyance.'
Neutron stars are so dense that a teaspoon of their material would weigh about six billion tons.
The human body contains enough iron to make a small nail, roughly 3 to 4 grams.
The word 'set' has the most definitions of any English word, with over 430 listed in the Oxford English Dictionary.
Strawberries are not true berries, but bananas, avocados, and watermelons are.
Russia spans 11 time zones, more than any other country on Earth.
The first email was sent by Ray Tomlinson in 1971, and he chose the @ symbol to separate the user name from the computer name.
Saffron is more expensive by weight than gold because each flower produces only three stigmas, which must be hand-picked.
Water can boil and freeze at the same time under specific conditions known as the triple point.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the construction of the Great Pyramid of Giza.
The Amazon rainforest produces about 20% of the world's oxygen.
The footprints left by the Apollo astronauts on the Moon will remain there for at least 100 million years because there is no wind or water to erode them.
Your stomach gets a new lining every three to four days to prevent it from digesting itself.
There is a language in Botswana called Taa that uses over 100 distinct click consonants.
The mimic octopus can impersonate over 15 different marine species, including lionfish, flatfish, and sea snakes.
Iceland has no mosquitoes despite being surrounded by water and having wetlands.
The QWERTY keyboard layout was designed in 1873 partly to prevent typewriter jams by separating commonly used letter pairs.
Peanuts are not nuts. They are legumes, related to beans and lentils.
Glass is technically an amorphous solid, not a liquid. The myth that old windows are thicker at the bottom is due to old manufacturing methods.
The ancient Romans used urine as mouthwash because it contains ammonia, which is a natural cleaning agent.
A single bolt of lightning contains enough energy to toast roughly 100,000 slices of bread.
The Sun accounts for 99.86% of all the mass in our solar system.
The human brain uses about 20% of the body's total energy, despite being only about 2% of its weight.
The sentence 'Buffalo buffalo Buffalo buffalo buffalo buffalo Buffalo buffalo' is grammatically correct in English.
Cows have best friends and become stressed when they are separated from them.
The Dead Sea is so salty that no macroscopic aquatic organisms can survive in it, and you can float effortlessly on its surface.
The Apollo 11 guidance computer had less processing power than a modern pocket calculator.
Vanilla is the second most expensive spice in the world after saffron because its orchid flowers must be hand-pollinated.
If you could fold a piece of paper 42 times, it would be thick enough to reach the Moon.
The Library of Alexandria, one of the largest and most significant libraries of the ancient world, was gradually destroyed over several centuries, not in a single fire.
Certain species of bamboo can grow up to 91 centimeters (about 36 inches) in a single day.
There are more stars in the observable universe than grains of sand on all of Earth's beaches.
Humans share about 60% of their DNA with bananas.
Mandarin Chinese is the most spoken language in the world by number of native speakers, with over 900 million.
The heart of a blue whale is so large that a small child could crawl through its arteries.
Mount Chimborazo in Ecuador, not Mount Everest, is the closest point on Earth's surface to the Sun due to Earth's equatorial bulge.
The first 1 GB hard drive, introduced by IBM in 1980, weighed about 250 kilograms and cost $40,000.
Ketchup was sold as medicine in the 1830s, marketed as a cure for diarrhea and indigestion.
Hot water can freeze faster than cold water under certain conditions, a phenomenon known as the Mpemba effect.
Oxford University is older than the Aztec Empire. Teaching at Oxford began around 1096, while the Aztec civilization started in 1325.
A teaspoon of soil contains more microorganisms than there are people on Earth.
The Voyager 1 spacecraft, launched in 1977, is the most distant human-made object from Earth, now in interstellar space.
The cornea is one of only two parts of the human body with no blood supply; it receives oxygen directly from the air.
There are about 7,000 languages spoken in the world today, but nearly half of them are expected to disappear by the end of this century.
Sea otters hold hands while sleeping to keep from drifting apart.
Canada has more lakes than the rest of the world's countries combined.
The entire text of Wikipedia in English could fit on a 22 GB file, small enough for a single USB flash drive.
Apples float in water because they are 25% air.
Light from the Sun takes about 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth.
Ancient Egyptians used slabs of stone as pillows.
The Amazon River dolphin (boto) can turn its head 90 degrees because its cervical vertebrae are not fused.
Saturn's density is low enough that it would float if placed in a sufficiently large body of water.
The average adult human skeleton has 206 bones, but babies are born with roughly 270, many of which fuse together during growth.
The longest word in English without a repeating letter is 'uncopyrightable,' at 15 letters.
Dolphins sleep with one eye open and one half of their brain awake to stay alert for predators.
Africa is the only continent that spans all four hemispheres: north, south, east, and west.
More than 90% of the world's data was generated in the last two years.
Chocolate was used as currency by the Aztecs.
A photon of light can take up to 40,000 years to travel from the core of the Sun to its surface, but only about 8 minutes to travel from the surface to Earth.
The Rosetta Stone, discovered in 1799, was the key to deciphering Egyptian hieroglyphics because it contained the same text in three scripts.
Fungi make up a kingdom of life more closely related to animals than to plants.
If two pieces of the same type of metal touch in the vacuum of space, they will permanently bond together through a process called cold welding.
The total length of all blood vessels in the human body is about 100,000 kilometers, enough to circle the Earth more than twice.
The exclamation mark was originally called the 'note of admiration.'
An electric eel can produce a shock of up to 860 volts, enough to stun a horse.
The Mariana Trench, the deepest point in the ocean, reaches a depth of about 11,034 meters (36,201 feet).
The first website ever created, by Tim Berners-Lee in 1991, is still online at info.cern.ch.
Carrots were originally purple before Dutch growers cultivated the orange variety in the 17th century.
The speed of light in a vacuum is exactly 299,792,458 meters per second, and this value is used to define the length of a meter.
Viking warriors filed grooves into their teeth and filled them with colored resin, possibly as a form of intimidation or identity.
Trees communicate with each other through underground fungal networks, sometimes called the 'wood wide web.'
One million Earths could fit inside the Sun.
The surface area of the human lungs, if spread flat, would cover roughly the size of a tennis court.
The word 'alphabet' comes from the first two letters of the Greek alphabet: alpha and beta.
A mantis shrimp can punch with the force of a .22 caliber bullet and can see 16 types of color receptors, compared to humans' three.
The Sahara Desert is roughly the same size as the entire United States.
The first mobile phone call was made on April 3, 1973, by Motorola engineer Martin Cooper.
Cashews grow on the bottom of cashew apples, and the shells contain a toxic oil related to poison ivy.
Helium is the only element that was discovered in space before it was found on Earth, first detected in the Sun's spectrum in 1868.
During the construction of the Great Pyramid of Giza, mammoths were still alive on Wrangel Island in the Arctic.
The Great Barrier Reef is the largest living structure on Earth, visible from space, stretching over 2,300 kilometers.
A year on Mercury lasts only 88 Earth days, but a single day on Mercury (sunrise to sunrise) lasts about 176 Earth days.
Human teeth are the only part of the body that cannot repair themselves.
The Hawaiian language has only 13 letters: five vowels and eight consonants.
Tardigrades, also known as water bears, can survive in the vacuum of outer space, extreme radiation, and temperatures from near absolute zero to 150 degrees Celsius.
The continent of Antarctica has no permanent human residents and no native land mammals.
GPS satellites must account for Einstein's theory of relativity; without corrections, GPS positions would drift by about 10 kilometers per day.
Lobsters were once considered food for the poor and were served to prisoners and servants in colonial America.
If the Sun were the size of a typical front door, Earth would be the size of a nickel.
The human eye can distinguish approximately 10 million different colors.
The dot over the lowercase letters 'i' and 'j' is called a tittle.
